title: Planera parvifolia
taxonomy: Planera parvifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. It is native to Central and South America and is also known as the small-leaved planera. This plant is a part of the family Melastomataceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the "membracine family." The family Melastomataceae is a part of the larger clade of flowering plants known as the asterids. The asterids are a group of flowering plants that also include the families of plants such as the Ericaceae, the Gentianaceae, and the Rubiaceae.

intro: Planera parvifolia, also known as American bladdernut, is a perennial plant species native to Eastern North America, characterized by its unique, yellow-green flowers and bladdery fruit. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Planera parvifolia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
